claim: Reviewed Codex's P1-deepening slice (untracked at the time of review; renames + audit body + tests + selftest next_command + roadmap doc refs). Direction-first gate implemented exactly as steered: INCREASE FAIL, DECREASE PASS, NEW unallowlisted triple FAIL, STABLE+touched needs Migration-intent: sm83_shared_tables_hold marker, STABLE+untouched PASS, count-to-zero PASS so the allowlist can shrink. AST handling is clean: Name/Attribute/Call paths all counted exactly once (visit_Call short-circuits to avoid double-counting via child Name walk), async function defs handled, scope tracking via push/pop on Class/Function entry. Touched-detection uses three diff sources (working, staged, HEAD^..HEAD) covering the common slice workflow. Tests cover all seven acceptance cases (a-g from the corrected spec) plus a live-repo no-op stability case and a real git-init fixture that drives _changed_line_ranges end-to-end. Dispatch-family taxonomy (register_index/control_flow/cpu_state) is the right grouping for the seven SHARED_TABLE_NAMES. ALLOWLIST has 15 entries pinning the exact current counts in effect_trace.py and dynamic_taint.py. One non-blocking observation: the new audit counts USES not REASSIGNMENTS, so a module-level INDEX_REG=other_dict shadow would slip past unless a function uses the redefined value (in which case the per-function dispatch-site gate catches it downstream). Narrow surface, not blocking acceptance. Codex's ack_start was missing write_set/safe_write_set_for_other/collision_risk_files; they self-corrected the protocol in their reply and will declare write sets before implementation going forward. No proof-boundary regression, no save-format change, no asm touched.

claim: Codex starts a debugger bug-fix slice for the stale trace-state probe failure: add a big-endian symbol-word helper for Pokemon HP words and wire the state probe to use it, preserving the existing little-endian read_symbol_word for pointer-like fields.

Ranking slice ready: tools/debugger/ranking.py now carries proof_status_by_source structured maps alongside the text-string proof_status field, mirroring the causal_graph (ebdb943b) and visualization (87d80b22) precedent. Two surfaces: (1) finding() writes a per-finding proof_status_by_source dict from merged evidence_atoms via inlined bank_state_record_proof_status_by_source helper; (2) rank_findings adds a top-level proof_status_by_source aggregated across all findings via new proof_status_by_source_summary helper. Key format matches precedent: bank_state:{group}:{name}:{source}. No proof promotion (strongest_proof_status reduction only). Inlined the helper rather than importing from causal_graph because ranking is loaded early in __init__.py via reporting->ranking, before causal_graph's transitive deps (coverage, localize, mirrors, content_scenarios, content_mirror) finish loading; the cycle would crash debugger imports. ranking.py already has parallel inline copies of bank_state_record_groups and bank_state_record_group_name from the same precedent, so this follows local convention. Hoist of the helper to tools/debugger/evidence.py is queued for the slice where impact.py and playtest_packet.py become the third + fourth consumers (Codex's planned follow-on). Test added in test_p0_proof_boundaries.py extending the existing reverse-query fixture: two new assertions for per-finding ranked_reverse[proof_status_by_source] and top-level ranked[proof_status_by_source]. Test count went from 749 to 751.
